summary: Raised item translation-unit match coverage toward the PR goal "All item TUs >95%" by matching or improving many item files, updating headers/struct types, localizing symbol-map entries, and switching several item TUs from NonMatching to Matching. The decomp.dev GALE01 report recorded +7,840 matched-code bytes, +9,756 linked-code bytes, 26 new matches, and 22 improvements in still-unmatched items.

key_files.role: Changed itlgunbeam.c, itgamewatchjudge.c, itoldkuri.c, and itgreatfoxlaser.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Marked itLgunbeam_UnkMotion0_* functions and many string/float/double constants as scope:local @NNN symbols instead of global it_80xxxx symbols.
Large common item-spawn/allocator matching pass: typed weighted-pick helpers, bit-mask loops, stack/register tuning, and removal of large inactive alternatives.
Updated it_8026CA4C signature from u16* to HSD_ObjAllocUnk6*.
Corrected Mewtwo Shadow Ball item-var field types and split itMewtwoShadowball_DatAttrs x1C array into explicit scalar fields.
Modeled ItemCommonData.xFC as a float array and named monster item count fields x128-x134.
Implemented previously NOT_IMPLEMENTED link-chain routines it_802A6474 and it_802A7384.
Matched Ness yoyo link creation, cleanup, chain physics/collision helpers, and JObj transform updates.
Updated it_802BF4A0 to return s32 and it_802BF800 to return bool.
Improved Shadow Ball spawn/update/charge code using corrected attrs, parameter ordering, mtx helpers, and scale/charge logic.
Documented corrected angle/facing_dir ordering for it_802C519C.
Matched L-gun beam angle clamp/reflection/collision/spawn code with local static callbacks.
Added old Kuriboh state table, initialization behavior, and matched it_2725_Logic0_DmgReceived.
Added item state table and simplified Judge orientation/removal logic through shared destroyed callback.
Added state table and matched Great Fox laser spawn setup including scale and rotation side-effect.
Introduced state-desc helper, tier pointer temporaries, bitfield tweaks, and PAD_STACK changes.
Cleaned Bomb Hei animation temporaries for a closer match.
Removed TEST_REAL branches, added inline wrappers and dummy matching structure for itLinkbomb_UnkMotion3_Anim.
Minor temp/PAD_STACK reordering for boomerang routines.
Added a LoadJoint inline wrapper and small stack tuning for sea chain link creation.

what_changed: Converted four item translation units in configure.py to Matching: itlgunbeam.c, itgamewatchjudge.c, itoldkuri.c, and itgreatfoxlaser.c.
Updated symbols.txt so L-gun beam UnkMotion0 callbacks and many data/sdata/sdata2 constants are local symbols, often using compiler-style @NNN labels.

Reworked it_266F common item code around weighted item-pick tables, random spawn timing, collision helpers, and callback invocation, including it_8026CA4C, it_8026CB9C, it_8026CD50, it_8026CF04, and it_8026D018.
Corrected struct/header modeling: Mewtwo Shadow Ball item vars and dat attrs, ItemCommonData.xFC as a float range table, and HSD_ObjAllocUnk6 arguments for it_8026CA4C.
Implemented missing Link hookshot chain-positioning and JObj-orientation routines it_802A6474 and it_802A7384, lifting them from NOT_IMPLEMENTED to high partial matches per the bot report.
Added or surfaced ItemStateTable definitions for GameWatch Judge, Great Fox Laser, and old Kuriboh, contributing to matched data.

Improved item-specific codegen across L-gun beam, Link bomb, Link boomerang, Bomb Hei, item 2E5A, Mewtwo Shadow Ball, Ness yoyo, old Kuriboh, and sea chain through tighter temporaries, helper inlines, PAD_STACK adjustments, and corrected bool/int signatures.

decomp.dev reported 26 new matches, including oldkuri damage/data, several Ness yoyo functions, L-gun beam routines, GameWatch Judge data/functions, Great Fox laser data/functions, and small final-byte matches in it_266F and Mewtwo Shadow Ball.
smart_moves: Used symbol visibility as a matching tool: private item callbacks/constants were marked local rather than kept as global it_80xxxx symbols.
Replaced vague filler and wrong scalar types with fields that match actual access patterns, especially ItemCommonData.xFC and Mewtwo Shadow Ball attr fields x1C/x20/x24/x28/x2C.

Factored repeated code into small static inline helpers such as clamp_angle, it_2E5A_ApplyStateDesc, it_802BE65C_LoadString, and it_802BAF2C_LoadX64 to preserve repeated source structure without adding exported symbols.
Used explicit block scopes, reordered temporaries, PAD_STACK sizes, and dummy locals/branches where needed to steer Metrowerks stack/register allocation.
Modeled contiguous globals in it_8026CF04 with a local Block overlay over it_804A0E30/it_804A0E50/it_804A0E60 instead of fighting misleading individual-global access patterns.
For chain items, reused existing ItemLink geometry/JObj helpers and expressed segment length clamping directly, which made Link hookshot and Ness yoyo code much more matchable.
decomp_lessons: Header accuracy matters: one wrong field type or argument order can hold back many item functions; this PR fixed f32/u32/s32 fields and angle/facing_dir ordering in Shadow Ball APIs.
Compiler-local data should be represented as local @NNN symbols in symbols.txt when it is not actually exported; leaving those as global address names can block data matches.
When a helper logically fills a struct field, give it the correct struct-pointer type, as in it_8026CA4C(HSD_ObjAllocUnk6*) writing x8, instead of treating it as a u16 buffer.
A bool-returning helper may still carry a multi-bit collision mask in codegen; it_8026E_inline returns cond and callers explicitly test res & 0xF.

Do not delete strange-looking matching artifacts too quickly: the diff preserves comments like "permuterslop" and "cursed, wtf" around dummy null checks or unused comparisons that were likely required for codegen.
ABS-style expressions can match better than manual sign-flip blocks, as seen in item collision/velocity cleanup.
State tables and special-attribute structs often unlock both data and code matches; defining ItemStateTable arrays in C contributed directly to .data matches for several item files.
Large NOT_IMPLEMENTED chain routines can be approached by identifying shared ItemLink primitives first, then matching segment traversal and length constraints.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions keep address-based names such as it_8026CA4C, it_802A6474, it_802BF4A0, and it_802C53F0.
Item callbacks follow existing item/motion naming: ItemName_UnkMotionN_Anim, _Phys, _Coll, and LogicNN_* names such as itLgunbeam_UnkMotion0_Coll and it_2725_Logic0_DmgReceived.
Global data/state tables remain address-based when emitted in C, e.g. ItemStateTable it_803F7968, it_803F8FD0, and it_803F8320.
Small local helpers are named descriptively but module-scoped, e.g. clamp_angle, it_2E5A_ApplyStateDesc, it_802BE65C_LoadString, and it_802BAF2C_LoadX64.
Compiler-local constants in symbols.txt use @NNN local labels rather than global it_804Dxxxx names when evidence shows local scope.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Applied #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on it_8026CA4C to preserve a needed call boundary.
Adjusted PAD_STACK values in many routines, including it_266F, it_2E5A, itlgunbeam, itlinkboomerang, itnessyoyo, and itmewtwoshadowball.
Used explicit __shr2u-based 64-bit mask shifts in it_8026CD50 while simplifying clearer low/high mask variable names.
Introduced helper inlines to force repeated expansion patterns without exporting extra functions.
Used pointer temporaries and local variable lifetimes to influence register allocation, e.g. tier pointers in it_2E5A and range pointers in it_266F.
Changed GET_ITEM casts away from unnecessary HSD_GObj* casts where typed Item_GObj* arguments already matched expected codegen.

Inserted or retained harmless side effects such as HSD_JObjGetRotation into an unused Quaternion in itgreatfoxlaser and an unused equality check in fn_802C5E18 because the diff indicates they affected matching.
Changed bool/int/s32 return types and callback signatures where codegen required exact ABI behavior, notably it_802BF4A0, it_802BF800, and L-gun beam flag arguments.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the PR slice.
decomp.dev bot GALE01 report: matched code 65.25% (+0.20%, +7840 bytes), linked code 37.33% (+0.25%, +9756 bytes), matched data 38.60% (+0.03%, +376 bytes), linked data 32.89% (+0.04%, +480 bytes).

Bot report listed 26 new matches and 22 improvements in unmatched items; the largest unmatched-item improvements were itlinkhookshot it_802A7384 (+750 bytes, 0.73% to 97.80%) and it_802A6474 (+681 bytes, 0.71% to 86.72%).
follow_up_queries: Find PR 2438 examples of using #pragma dont_inline for a single helper while keeping the TU matching.
Search it_8026CA4C HSD_ObjAllocUnk6 signature when future item weighted-pick tables miscompile.
Look up PR 2438 Mewtwo Shadow Ball attr split when x1C array indexing blocks matches.
Use PR 2438 itlinkhookshot it_802A6474 and it_802A7384 as references for ItemLink chain traversal and segment length clamping.
Search PR 2438 symbols.txt @NNN local constants before deciding whether an it_804Dxxxx sdata2 symbol should be global.
Use Ness yoyo PR 2438 changes for bool vs s32 return-type corrections in chain physics helpers.
Compare L-gun beam clamp_angle and collision flag handling when angle normalization loops mismatch.
